ddls:
CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each measurement location */,
  "x_coordinates" REAL /* X-coordinate of the measurement site */,
  "y_coordinates" REAL /* Y-coordinate of the measurement site */,
  "site_name" TEXT /* Name of the measurement site */,
  "region" TEXT /* Geographical region of the site */,
  "altitude" REAL /* Altitude of the measurement site */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the site is located */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "measurements" (
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each measurement */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location where the measurement was taken */,
  "tep" REAL /* Temperature Efficiency */,
  "ted" REAL /* Temperature Differential */,
  "times" REAL /* Timestamp for the measurement */,
  "high" REAL /* High temperature recorded */,
  "low" REAL /* Low temperature recorded */,
  "energy_production" REAL /* Energy production metric */,
  "measurement_unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("measurement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_measurements_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, analyst) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login timestamp */,
  "organization" TEXT /* Organization the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* ID of the measurement being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the measurement */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the measurement was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent of the device used */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_measurement_id FOREIGN KEY ("measurement_id") REFERENCES measurements ("measurement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "environmental_conditions" (
  "condition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each environmental condition entry */,
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the measurement for which the conditions apply */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Humidity at the time of measurement */,
  "air_pressure" REAL /* Air pressure at the time of measurement */,
  "wind_speed" REAL /* Wind speed at the time of measurement */,
  "precipitation" REAL /* Precipitation level */,
  "soil_moisture" REAL /* Soil moisture content */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("condition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_environmental_conditions_measurement_id FOREIGN KEY ("measurement_id") REFERENCES measurements ("measurement_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "projects" (
  "project_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each project */,
  "project_name" TEXT /* Name of the project */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the project */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the project */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the project */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget allocated for the project */,
  "principal_investigator" TEXT /* Lead researcher or investigator */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("project_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "project_measurements" (
  "pm_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each project-measurement association */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project */,
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* ID of the measurement associated with the project */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("pm_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_project_measurements_project_id FOREIGN KEY ("project_id") REFERENCES projects ("project_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_project_measurements_measurement_id FOREIGN KEY ("measurement_id") REFERENCES measurements ("measurement_id")
)
